headline: Asian shares gain with most markets closed for May Day, while oil holds steady at $111 a barrel
summary: Brent crude’s price held steady at $111.66 per barrel while U.S. benchmark crude oil added 46 cents to $105.53 a barrel.  Prospects for a deal to cement a three-week ceasefire in the Iran wa r remained clouded as Iran’s supreme leader said it will protect its nuclear and missile capabilities as a national asset.  In Tokyo, the Nikkei 225 gained 0.7% to 59,687.65 as the Japanese yen gained against the U.S. dollar.

content.title: Asian shares gain with most markets closed for May Day, while oil holds steady at $111 a barrel
content.summary: Brent crude’s price held steady at $111.66 per barrel while U.S. benchmark crude oil added 46 cents to $105.53 a barrel.  Prospects for a deal to cement a three-week ceasefire in the Iran wa r remained clouded as Iran’s supreme leader said it will protect its nuclear and missile capabilities as a national asset.  In Tokyo, the Nikkei 225 gained 0.7% to 59,687.65 as the Japanese yen gained against the U.S. dollar.

thumbnail.caption: A person walks in front of an electronic stock board showing Japan's Nikkei index at a securities firm Thursday, April 30, 2026, in Tokyo. (AP Photo/Eugene Hoshiko)

headline: Has CAT Stock Run Ahead Of Its Valuation?
summary: The defining feature of Caterpillar's (CAT) Q1 2026 performance is the decoupling of its valuation from the traditional heavy machinery cycle. At the April 30 close price of $890 and a forward earnings estimate of $23.43, the stock is trading at 38 times earnings. This multiple indicates that the market has transitioned from viewing Caterpillar as a cyclical industrial to a structural play on the AI data center buildout. The $63 billion backlog, which grew 79% year over year, is the primary evid

headline: Leading Machinery Stock Caterpillar's Composite Rating Surges To 98 On Q1 Earnings
summary: Caterpillar saw its IBD SmartSelect Composite Rating rise to 98 Friday, up from 93 the day before.  The revised score means the stock currently tops 98% of all other stocks in terms of key performance metrics and technical strength.  Is Caterpillar Stock A Buy?

headline: Morgan Stanley Doubles Caterpillar Price Target to $915: Is This the Most Underrated Industrial of 2026?
summary: Morgan Stanley raised its price target on Caterpillar (NYSE:CAT) to $915 from $430, while upgrading the stock to Equal Weight from Underweight. The dramatic shift, which effectively more than doubles the prior target, reflects what the firm calls “ongoing strong market execution, a robust backlog, and demand growth.” JPMorgan Chase went further, lifting its target ... Morgan Stanley Doubles Caterpillar Price Target to $915: Is This the Most Underrated Industrial of 2026?
